question.text: Under Section 4 of PMLA, if the proceeds of crime relate to offences under the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances Act, 1985, the maximum punishment may extend to:

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** While the standard punishment under Section 4 is 3-7 years, when the proceeds of crime relate to offences specified under Paragraph 2 of Part A of the Schedule (NDPS Act offences), the maximum extends from 7 years to 10 years.
**The Trap:** The default maximum is 7 years; the enhanced maximum for NDPS-related ML is 10 years. Students may confuse these two limits. There is no life imprisonment provision under Section 4.
**Cross-Exam Context:** This enhanced punishment for narcotics-related ML reflects the global approach (Vienna Convention 1988 specifically targeted drug trafficking proceeds). NISM XXIV expects you to know this distinction.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The enhanced punishment for NDPS-related offences shows a hierarchy of severity in PMLA. Drug money laundering is treated more harshly than other forms — a principle consistent with FATF recommendations.
